_meta.note: Daemon-backed scoped rule deactivation. scope is REQUIRED: it is present in the MCP input schema required[] (TB-5), so an omitted scope is rejected at the wire-form boundary, never silently widened to global. This fixture uses explicit global scope. TB-6: a deactivate that matches no active (rule_id, version, scope) row is NOT a silent ok; it returns a typed RuleNotActive teaching error that echoes the actually-active version(s).

rule_not_active_error_example.message: no active row for rule 'rust-compile-error' v99 scope global; active version is 1

invariants: scope is required and present in the MCP input schema required[] (TB-5)
an omitted scope is rejected, never silently widened to global
jobs_rebound is a count, not job output
success payload reports whether an active binding was closed
a deactivate matching no active (rule_id, version, scope) row returns a typed RuleNotActive error, not a silent ok:true/was_deactivated:false
the RuleNotActive message echoes the version(s) actually active under the scope so the caller can self-correct
